input.headline: Manchester derby live on Sky in January as fixtures released up to February 2

raw.headline: Manchester derby live on Sky in January as fixtures released up to February 2

winner: The Manchester Derby is so important that Sky is airing it... in January. Apparently, even time zones respect the intensity of two clubs that can't agree on which side of a river they're from.

candidates.text: Why did the Manchester derby player bring a ladder to the match? Because he heard the fixtures were *raised* to new heights!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative wordplay on 'raised' with a sports context, concise and specific to the headline topic."}

candidates.text: I heard Sky is broadcasting the Manchester derby with such clarity now, you can almost hear the players' heartbeats—unless they're running too fast for the signal to catch up.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific comparison to signal quality, with a clever twist on technical limitations."}

candidates.text: I told the TV schedule they were putting Manchester's best games on hold for January—turns out they just meant the *Derby* was moving to a later date!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Funny wordplay on 'Derby' being moved, concise and specific to the headline with a surprising twist."}

candidates.text: The Manchester Derby is so important that Sky is airing it... in January. Apparently, even time zones respect the intensity of two clubs that can't agree on which side of a river they're from.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on time zones and river sides, specific to Manchester derby, concise and funny."}

candidates.text: Sky Sports announced they'd be showing the Manchester derby in January—then their CEO said he'd be watching it from February.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke has a mild twist with the CEO's comment, but it lacks specificity and surprise, making it somewhat generic."}
